key_insight: The paper builds a neural-spectral control solver for wave equations by learning only the time-varying modal coefficients in a Dirichlet spectral basis, preserving the equation’s oscillatory structure while approximating controls and states in the correct energy norms.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical path for PDE-constrained optimization in wave-dominated systems where standard time-stepping can distort high-frequency dynamics and degrade control quality.
method: Expand state and control in a Dirichlet spectral basis to encode spatial wave structure.
Use shallow neural networks to parameterize the time-dependent modal coefficients.
Analyze approximation/error sources from spectral truncation, neural approximation, quadrature, and optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports approximation theorems plus numerical experiments in 1D, 2D, and 3D on nonlinear, linear-reference, and high-dimensional control benchmarks.
watch_item: Performance depends on spectral truncation and optimization quality, and the method still assumes an exact control exists in the relevant energy framework.
paper_title: A Structure-Preserving Neural-Spectral Method for Reconstructing Controls of Wave Equations
